testResults.name: T10551 ADR-073 PM-Core V2 doctrine acceptance evidence

assertionResults.fullName: AC1 ADR-073 makes Saga a first-class type discriminator, not label-overlay doctrine
assertionResults.title: ADR-073 lines 41-47, 61, 65, and 79-80 state canonical storage is tasks.type='saga' and labels never define hierarchy; ADR-073 contains no 'label-overlay' or 'label overlay' doctrine

assertionResults.fullName: AC2 ADR-073 explicitly deprecates legacy groups hierarchy semantics
assertionResults.title: ADR-073 lines 110-116 and 259-266 state parent_id is the only containment edge and task_relations.type='groups' is legacy non-containment provenance/cross-reference only

assertionResults.fullName: AC3 ADR-073 pins parent depth semantics to parent-edge distance
assertionResults.title: ADR-073 lines 122-128 define hierarchy.maxDepth=3 as the maximum number of parent_id edges and pin Saga/Epic/Task/Subtask depths 0/1/2/3
INVARIANTS.md line 37 records I5 as 'Parent containment only; legacy groups hierarchy deprecated' and says groups must not drive child listing, rollups, completion, or depth

assertionResults.fullName: AC5 docs/registry/INVARIANTS.md mirrors maxDepth parent-edge budget
assertionResults.title: INVARIANTS.md line 39 records I7 as maximum parent depth 3 with Saga(depth 0) → Epic(depth 1) → Task(depth 2) → Subtask(depth 3), and says secondary relations cannot extend or bypass the budget

metadata.acceptanceScope: ADR/doctrine acceptance checks for the T10551 amendment commit; historical changelog/archive text was not treated as current doctrine.
